title: Meat-Filled Hunan Dough Dumplings
description: A hearty, diabetic-friendly Chinese dish from the Hunan province featuring green curry paste and a dense, buttery dough. Serve with your favorite dipping sauce.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground pork, green curry paste, salt, and black pepper. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the mixture with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
On a lightly floured surface, lay out a wonton wrapper. Place a tablespoon of the meat mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with a small amount of water, then fold the wrapper into a half-moon shape, pressing the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and meat mixture.
In a large skillet or wok, melt 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter over medium-high heat. Add the dumplings and cook until the bottoms are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Add 1 cup of water to the skillet and cover with a lid. Steam the dumplings for 8-10 minutes, or until the filling is cooked through and the wrappers are tender.
In a small bowl, mix together the remaining 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter, soy sauce, sesame oil, and chopped scallions. Serve the dumplings with the sauce spooned over the top.
Garnish with chopped cilantro, if desired.
Serve hot, with your favorite dipping sauce on the side.
These dumplings are best served immediately, but they can be refrigerated or frozen for later use.
